seo.title: Building Data Extraction with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LangGraph enables building complex, stateful data extraction workflows from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls in cyclical agent graphs. Guardrails are essential to prevent cyclic execution loops, state corruption during parallel extractions, and ensure extracted data meets schema compliance and accuracy requirements. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before they cascade in production pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
The typical LangGraph data extraction architecture starts with a document loader node that ingests unstructured text (PDFs, emails, reports). Router nodes then dispatch to parallel extraction branches for different schema sections (e.g., entities, dates, amounts), each invoking schema-guided LLMs. Results merge at a validator node that checks completeness and type consistency before human-in-the-loop review or final output.

State is managed via LangGraph's checkpointing system, persisting extraction progress across cycles. Conditional edges handle retries for low-confidence extractions, while parallel branches enable efficient multi-field extraction. The graph cycles back for refinement when validation fails schema constraints.

This cyclical design maximizes extraction accuracy but introduces risks like infinite loops from misconfigured retry conditions and state corruption when merging conflicting parallel results.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: Misconfigured retry conditions on low-confidence extractions trigger endless cycles
risks.mitigation: Wrap invoke() with vex.verify() to detect loop thresholds

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: Entity extractor and amount extractor produce conflicting values for same field
risks.mitigation: Add Vex merge node to validate field consistency before state update

risks.name: Checkpoint deserialization errors after schema changes
risks.description: Evolving extraction schemas break compatibility with saved checkpoints
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation prevents deserialization of incompatible states

risks.name: Human-in-the-loop breakpoints bypassed by conditional edges
risks.description: High-confidence paths skip required human review for critical fields
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces breakpoint execution regardless of confidence routing

implementation.title: Basic Schema-Guided Extraction Graph
implementation.description: Core LangGraph workflow for extracting structured data from documents using Pydantic schemas
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

class ExtractionState(TypedDict):
    document: str
    entities: dict
    confidence: float
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]

class Entity(BaseModel):
    name: str = Field(..., description="Person/organization name")
    role: str = Field(..., description="Role/position")

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

def extract_entities(state: ExtractionState):
    prompt = f"""Extract entities from: {state['document']}
Output JSON only."""
    chain = llm.with_structured_output(Entity, method="json_mode")
    result = chain.invoke(prompt)
    return {"entities": result.dict(), "confidence": 0.85}

def router(state: ExtractionState):
    return "end" if state['confidence'] > 0.8 else "retry"

workflow = StateGraph(state_schema=ExtractionState)
workflow.add_node("extract", extract_entities)
workflow.add_node("retry", lambda x: x)
workflow.set_entry_point("extract")
workflow.add_conditional_edges("extract", router, {"retry": "retry", "end": END})
app = workflow.compile()

implementation.title: Parallel Multi-Field Extraction
implementation.description: Extract multiple schema sections in parallel branches for efficiency
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Dict, Any
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END

class ExtractionState(TypedDict):
    document: str
    entities: dict
    dates: dict
    amounts: dict
    complete: bool

async def extract_entities(state):
    # Mock LLM extraction
    return {"entities": {"John Doe": "CEO"}}

async def extract_dates(state):
    return {"dates": {"report_date": "2024-01-15"}}

async def extract_amounts(state):
    return {"amounts": {"revenue": 1250000}}

def merge_results(state):
    return {"complete": True}

graph = StateGraph(ExtractionState)

# Add parallel extraction nodes
graph.add_node("entities", extract_entities)
graph.add_node("dates", extract_dates)
graph.add_node("amounts", extract_amounts)
graph.add_node("merge", merge_results)

# Fan-out to parallel extraction
graph.set_entry_point("entities")
graph.add_edge("entities", "dates")
graph.add_edge("dates", "amounts")
graph.add_edge("amounts", "merge")
graph.add_edge("merge", END)

app = graph.compile()

implementation.title: Confidence-Based Retry Loop
implementation.description: Cyclical refinement for low-confidence extractions with max iterations
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END

class ExtractionState(TypedDict):
    document: str
    result: dict
    confidence: float
    attempts: int

MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3

def extract_with_retry(state: ExtractionState):
    attempts = state.get('attempts', 0) + 1
    confidence = 0.7 + (0.1 * attempts)  # Mock improvement
    
    return {
        'result': {'entities': ['John Doe']},
        'confidence': min(confidence, 0.95),
        'attempts': attempts
    }

def should_retry(state: ExtractionState):
    return "retry" if state['confidence'] < 0.9 and state['attempts'] < MAX_ATTEMPTS else END

graph = StateGraph(ExtractionState)
graph.add_node("extract", extract_with_retry)
graph.set_entry_point("extract")
graph.add_conditional_edges("extract", should_retry)
app = graph.compile()

implementation.title: Pydantic Schema Enforcement
implementation.description: Strict type validation using Pydantic models for extraction output

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Catch cyclic execution and schema violations at the graph boundary
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver

# Your compiled LangGraph app
app = workflow.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver())

# Vex verification spec for data extraction
verify_spec = {
    "name": "data_extraction",
    "asserts": [
        {"type": "schema_compliance", "schema": Invoice.model_json_schema()},
        {"type": "field_accuracy", "fields": ["invoice_number", "total"], "min_confidence": 0.9},
        {"type": "completeness", "required_fields": ["line_items", "date"]},
        {"type": "no_cycles", "max_iterations": 10}
    ]
}

# Wrap invoke with Vex
result = vex.verify(
    app.invoke,
    config=verify_spec,
    input={"document": "Invoice INV-123456 dated 2024-01-15 total $1250..."},
    threshold="medium"
)

if result['verified']:
    print('Extraction verified:', result['output'])
else:
    print('Blocked:', result['failures'])
